title: Limits of BLEU
concept: BLEU is sensitive to exact word choice and spelling; it does not understand synonyms or paraphrasing, sometimes penalizing valid translations.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of the BLEU metric in machine translation?

options.text: To automatically score machine translations by comparing them to human translations.
To translate text from one language to another in real-time.
To check if a paragraph has spelling errors.

exercises.explain: BLEU stands for Bilingual Evaluation Understudy. It is a quick, automated way to evaluate machine translations by matching words with a reference text.

cards.title: The Robot Critic
cards.body: Imagine translating a beautiful poem, but a computer tells you it's completely wrong just because you used the word 'sofa' instead of 'couch'. Today, we will explore why the popular BLEU metric often struggles with the beauty of human language!

cards.title: What is the BLEU Limit?
cards.body: BLEU measures translation quality by counting matching words between the AI's translation and a human reference. However, it cannot understand meaning. If an AI uses a perfect synonym that isn't in the reference list, BLEU marks it as incorrect.

cards.title: The Strict Teacher
cards.body: Imagine a strict school teacher who only accepts answers that match their answer key word-for-word. If you write 'tiny' instead of 'small', they mark it wrong, even though both mean the same thing. BLEU is just like that strict teacher!

cards.title: A Perfect But "Failed" Translation
cards.body: If the human reference is "The dog is happy," and the AI writes "The pup is joyful," BLEU gives this a score of 0% because none of the words match exactly, even though the translation is excellent!

cards.title: Myth: Low Score = Bad Translation
cards.body: Many people think a low BLEU score always means a translation is bad. In reality, a translation can be natural, fluent, and accurate, but still get a low BLEU score simply because it uses different words than the reference text.

cards.title: Exact Match vs. Real Meaning
cards.body: See how BLEU grades based on exact matches rather than the actual meaning of the sentences.

left.text: Looks for exact word-for-word spelling matches. Penalizes synonyms.

right.text: Understands meaning, context, and accepts different ways to say the same thing.

exercises.prompt: What is the main limitation of the BLEU evaluation metric?

options.text: It is too slow to calculate on modern computers.
It relies on exact word matches and does not understand synonyms.
It only works for translations between English and Spanish.
It requires human judges to rate every sentence manually.

exercises.explain: BLEU looks for exact spelling matches. It does not understand context, meaning, or synonyms, so it struggles with creative paraphrasing.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A small spelling mistake in a translation can significantly lower its BLEU score.
exercises.explain: Because BLEU matches words letter-for-letter, a simple typo like 'receeve' instead of 'receive' will prevent a match, lowering the score.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the sentence about BLEU's limitations.
exercises.sentence: BLEU cannot recognize ________, meaning it penalizes translations that use different words with the same meaning.
exercises.explain: BLEU looks for exact spelling matches. It does not know that words like 'huge' and 'large' mean the same thing.

exercises.prompt:
Reference: "The cat sat on the sofa."

Which of these translations is perfectly natural to humans, but will get a lower BLEU score because of synonym differences?

options.text: Translation A: "The cat sat on the sofa."
Translation B: "A kitten rested on the couch."
Both translations will get the exact same BLEU score.

exercises.explain: Option B is the correct translation, but because it uses 'couch' instead of 'sofa', BLEU cannot match the words and gives it a lower score than the grammatically incorrect Option A.

exercises.prompt:
Imagine a reference translation says: "The car is quick." 

An AI translates this as: "The automobile is fast." 

What BLEU score will this translation receive, and why?

options.text: 100% because the meaning is perfectly preserved.
Around 80% because it only missed a few grammar rules.
0% because not a single word matches the reference translation exactly.

exercises.explain: BLEU matches words exactly. A translation with different but correct synonyms like 'automobile' or 'fast' will get a score of zero if the reference translation only uses the words 'car' and 'quick'.
